name: Antwerp

overview.description: Antwerp is Belgium's largest city by area and its main port. It's globally known as a diamond trading centre, with a significant Indian business community (many from the Gujarati and Jain diaspora) concentrated around the Diamond District near Antwerp-Central station.
whyVisit: One of the world's largest diamond trading hubs
Antwerp-Centraal, considered one of the most beautiful railway stations in the world
Historic Grote Markt and Cathedral of Our Lady

attractions.name: Antwerp-Centraal Station
attractions.description: A landmark building nicknamed the 'Railway Cathedral'.

attractions.name: Grote Markt
attractions.description: Historic market square with guild houses and the Brabo Fountain.

attractions.name: Cathedral of Our Lady
attractions.description: Belgium's largest Gothic cathedral, home to Rubens masterpieces.

attractions.name: Diamond District
attractions.description: The world-famous diamond trading quarter, home to many Indian-run diamond businesses.

transportation.description: Extensive tram network including underground 'premetro' sections.
tips: Buy tickets via the De Lijn app for the best price

transportation.description: Antwerp-Centraal connects directly to Brussels (~45 min), Amsterdam and Rotterdam.

hotels.name: Near Antwerp-Centraal / Diamond District
hotels.notes: Convenient for business travellers and the diamond trade.

foodSpots.name: Indian & Jain restaurants near the Diamond District
foodSpots.notes: Several dedicated vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants serve the diamond trading community.

tips: Dutch is the primary language — English is widely spoken in business and tourist areas.
The Diamond District has visible security; carry ID when visiting.
Antwerp is very cycling-friendly — consider a bike rental for short trips.
